title: Provenance-gated leaderboard (every row: external-cohort number + code+data+split provenance)
context: The leaderboard is the public-facing summary of all accepted benchmark results. Its design choices directly determine whether the project advances or undermines honest benchmarking. Every leaderboard row must show an external-cohort metric (not internal CV) and link to its (code, data, split) provenance triple; rows without provenance or external validation are not listed. This is the provenance-gated leaderboard: it is generated as a projection of the accepted submissions and benchmark definition objects, not hand-maintained. NC-derived entries carry the inherited restriction label.
objective: Implement the provenance-gated leaderboard: a generated artifact (from accepted submissions and benchmark definition objects) where every row shows an external-cohort metric, links to the (code, data, split) provenance triple, and carries a re-run-passed marker; rows without provenance or an external-cohort number are not admitted; NC-derived entries carry the non-commercial restriction label; the leaderboard is published CC-BY-4.0 (non-NC rows) or with inherited NC restriction (NC-derived rows).
acceptanceCriteria: Every leaderboard row displays an external-cohort metric (not internal cross-validation performance) and links to the (code version, data version, split hash) provenance triple
Rows without a provenance triple or without an external-cohort number are not listed; the generator enforces this automatically and a CI test confirms no row violates the requirement
Each row carries a re-run-passed marker (from the submission-017 re-run gate receipt) indicating the numbers were independently verified to reproduce within tolerance
NC-derived entries (rows whose data includes COSMIC/OncoKB sources) carry the inherited non-commercial restriction label; the leaderboard is published CC-BY-4.0 for non-NC rows

The leaderboard is generated as a projection of the accepted submission records and benchmark definition objects, not hand-maintained; re-running the generator from the committed records produces the same leaderboard
Technical reviewer and domain reviewer sign-offs on the leaderboard format and generator are recorded
output: A merged PR delivering the provenance-gated leaderboard generator and the initial leaderboard (seeded with the M1 and M2 baseline results) where every row shows an external-cohort metric, links to provenance, and carries a re-run-passed marker.
